questions.title: What is the Semi-Major Axis (km) of Watchtower's orbit?
questions.description: Use the GPS telemetry to estimate the average altitude of the spacecraft and add Earth's radius (6 378 km).

answer.reason: The defender flies an MEO orbit with SMA = 10 000 km, giving an altitude of ~3 622 km.

questions.title: What is the Inclination (deg) of Watchtower's orbit?
questions.description: Read the maximum |latitude| from the GPS time series.

answer.reason: The orbit was tuned for ground-station coverage across the Madrid → Doha → Singapore → Perth → Auckland → Miami chain; the resulting 37.5° inclination still passes directly over the Hormuz / Arabian Sea AOI on the descending leg of orbit 1.

questions.title: Around which AOI did GPS spoofing occur?
questions.description: Plot the GPS lat/lon trace and identify when reported position is inconsistent with the magnetometer and orbit propagation. The deceptive fix lands at the antipode of the affected region.

reason: The spoofing region was a 500 km-radius sphere centred on (26.5°N, 56.5°E) at MEO altitude — directly over the Hormuz Strait.
The spoofing region was a 500 km-radius sphere centred on (26.5°N, 56.5°E) at MEO altitude — directly over the Hormuz Strait.

questions.title: Which sensor returned a hard fault that required a component reset?
questions.description: One sensor's APID stopped emitting fresh fixes mid-orbit. Cross-check against magnetometer health to distinguish the hard fault from environmental denial.

reason: The GPS Sensor enters Fault State 4 at t=5 400 s; the magnetometer remains healthy throughout, distinguishing a hardware fault from the earlier spoofing/jamming.
The GPS Sensor enters Fault State 4 at t=5 400 s; the magnetometer remains healthy throughout, distinguishing a hardware fault from the earlier spoofing/jamming.

questions.title: What was the symptom of the storage corruption event?
questions.description: After t≈4 200 s, downlinked imagery contains corrupted bytes. Identify the type of failure observed.

reason: The Spacecraft event 'Storage Corruption' applies a 10% corruption fraction at intensity 0.2 — bit-flips in stored imagery, observable on next downlink.

questions.title: What is the most appropriate immediate mitigation for the GPS spoofing event?
questions.description: While the spacecraft is inside the spoofing volume, what action keeps mission-critical positioning trustworthy?

options: Ephemeris rollback / dead-reckon from last good fix
reason: The hardware is healthy — the receiver is being fed a deceptive fix. Trust the last known orbit and dead-reckon out of the volume; cross-check against the magnetometer.
The hardware is healthy — the receiver is being fed a deceptive fix. Trust the last known orbit and dead-reckon out of the volume; cross-check against the magnetometer.

questions.title: What is the rogue spacecraft's call-sign?
questions.description: Slew the camera onto the rogue spacecraft and read the chassis text.

answer.reason: The rogue carries a 'PHANTOM' Text component on both faces of the chassis — visible from any approach angle in the camera image.

questions.title: What is the carrier frequency (MHz) of the rogue spacecraft's transmitter?
questions.description: Use the EM sensor to scan the spectrum and identify the strong off-nominal carrier.

answer.reason: The Rogue team / PHANTOM spacecraft is configured at 905 MHz; the EM sensor will resolve a strong carrier at that frequency during replay/jam phases.

questions.title: Which ground stations saw the broadcast downlink jam during AOI imaging passes?
questions.description: Multiple selection. Identify ground stations whose SNR drops below ~15 dB during the AOI passes (T_AOI_1 ± 90 s and T_AOI_2 ± 90 s).

reason: The two phase-2b AOI passes are at lat 26.5°N — orbit-2 ascending crosses near the Caribbean (in view of Miami) and orbit-2 descending crosses the Mediterranean band (in view of Madrid). Update this answer post-dry-run with the actual stations observed; jamming is broadcast across all blue frequencies.

questions.title: Which blue team was singled out for pulsed uplink jamming?
questions.description: One team's uplink ack-rate drops to ~75-85% during a single ground-station pass mid-mission while other teams remain unaffected. Which one?

reason: The rogue's pulsed uplink jam targets Blue Bravo's frequency only — Blue Alpha sees no degradation.
The rogue's pulsed uplink jam targets Blue Bravo's frequency only — Blue Alpha sees no degradation.

questions.title: Roughly what proportion of Blue Bravo's commands failed during the uplink-jam window?
questions.description: Compare commands sent vs. commands acknowledged on Blue Bravo's console during the jam window. Express as a percentage.

answer.reason: The pulse pattern (8 s ON / 32 s OFF, 20% duty cycle) drops ~15-25% of commands. Tune on/off seconds in cyber_defender.py if observed rate differs.

questions.title: Did the rogue replay any captured ciphertext against your team during the second half of the mission?
questions.description: Check the EM sensor for spurious uplink-traffic spikes on your team's frequency without a matching ground-station transmission.

reason: The rogue captures foreign uplinks during 6 000-9 000 s and replays them at random against every blue team between 9 500-14 500 s.
The rogue captures foreign uplinks during 6 000-9 000 s and replays them at random against every blue team between 9 500-14 500 s.

questions.title: Which APIDs showed Phase-3 telemetry tampering?
questions.description: Multiple selection. Compare suspect telemetry fields against magnetometer/orbit-prop ground truth and identify every APID whose payload contents drifted.

reason: Cyber events tamper APID 100 (Ping State field flips to 'FLAG-OWN') and APID 301 (GPS position zeroed).

questions.title: What action mitigates a successful uplink replay attack against your team?
questions.description: Pick the on-board action that invalidates any previously-captured ciphertext for your team going forward.

reason: Rotating the Caesar key changes the on-board cipher; any previously-captured ciphertext is no longer decodable on the spacecraft, so the replay fails.

questions.title: What action mitigates a sustained downlink jam over an AOI ground-station pass?
questions.description: Pick the action that restores a usable downlink at the next available pass.

options: Frequency hop via the telemetry command
Wait for the rogue to leave the area
reason: Hopping to a frequency outside the rogue's barrage moves the link off the jammed channel — the imagery downlinks cleanly on the next non-AOI pass.
Hopping to a frequency outside the rogue's barrage moves the link off the jammed channel — the imagery downlinks cleanly on the next non-AOI pass.

questions.title: What action clears a Cyber telemetry-tamper overlay (APID 100 / 301)?
questions.description: Both Phase-3 cyber overlays were authored with `Clear On Reset: true`. Which command removes them?

reason: Resetting the Computer clears every resettable cyber overlay on the asset (per docs/scenarios/events.md § Reset behavior).
